items.title: Unraveling rescue-like behaviors: Neural circuits driving prosocial aid in mice
items.author: Maojun Hong, Liuting Zou, Rongfeng K. Hu
items.content: <article>Rescue-like prosocial behaviors are critical for the survival and well-being of social species. Yet, the neural circuits underlying these behaviors remain poorly understood. Recently, four independent research groups discovered rescue-like behaviors in mice—where individuals assist an unresponsive conspecific—and identified the key neural mechanisms involved.</article>

items.title: Lights, contrast, action! Disentangling visually induced oscillations
items.author: Chockalingam Ramanathan, Julia Veit
items.content: <article>Gamma oscillations are ubiquitous in visual processing. But what do they really represent? In this issue of Neuron, Meyerolbersleben et al. show that well-defined oscillatory bursts in the mouse visual cortex reflect the processing of distinct features of natural scenes.</article>

items.title: Fibromyalgia pain may have a gut solution
items.author: Isabelle Lesmana, Cheng Qian, Elaine Y. Hsiao
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Cai et al. demonstrated that transferring fecal microbiota from fibromyalgia patients to mice confers pain hypersensitivity and depression-like behavior, which is reversed by fecal microbiota transplant from healthy donors. Fibromyalgia patients experience significant symptom relief after fecal microbiota transplantation.</article>
